idea-plugin.name: External Terminal Launcher
idea-plugin.description:
External Terminal Launcher — Open your favorite terminal from the IDE

Adds a top-toolbar button (and a Tools menu action) that opens an external terminal emulator at your project's root directory. Built terminal-agnostic, starting with Warp and growing from there.

Supported terminals

  • Warp — Windows, macOS, Linux
  • Windows Terminal — Windows
  • WezTerm — Windows, macOS, Linux
  • Alacritty — Windows, macOS, Linux
  • kitty — macOS, Linux

Features

  • One-click launch — toolbar button opens the terminal at the project root
  • Tab reuse — opens a new tab in an existing window when the terminal supports it
  • Custom executable path — point each terminal at a non-standard install location
  • Bindable — assign your own shortcut in Settings → Keymap (no default binding)

Setup

  1. Open Settings → Tools → External Terminal
  2. Pick your terminal and (optionally) a custom executable path
  3. Click the toolbar button to launch

action.description: Open the configured external terminal at the project root
